# awscc_healthlake_data_transformation_profile (Resource)

Creates a Data Transformation Profile in AWS HealthLake that converts healthcare data from a source format (such as C-CDA or CSV) into FHIR R4. A profile is immutable once created; to change its template content, replace the resource. Only its tags can be updated in place.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `profile_name` (String) The human-readable name of the profile.
- `source_format` (String) The source format that this profile converts from.

### Optional

- `kms_key_id` (String) The identifier (key ID or ARN) of a customer-managed KMS key used to encrypt the profile's template content at rest. If omitted, an AWS owned key is used.
- `profile_description` (String) A human-readable description of the profile's purpose.
- `source` (Attributes) The source from which to create the profile's initial template content. Exactly one of the members must be specified. Use StarterProfile (C-CDA only), ProfileMapping (C-CDA or CSV), or ExistingVersionedProfileId to clone an existing profile. Each produces a published profile. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source))
- `tags` (Attributes Set) An array of key-value pairs to apply to this profile. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the data transformation profile.
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `profile_id` (String) The unique, server-generated identifier of the profile (32-character lowercase hexadecimal).
- `target_format` (String) The target format that this profile converts to. Always FHIR_R4.

<a id="nestedatt--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source`

Optional:

- `existing_versioned_profile_id` (Attributes) Create the profile by cloning a specific version of an existing profile. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source--existing_versioned_profile_id))
- `profile_mapping` (Attributes) Create the profile from raw Velocity template mapping content. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source--profile_mapping))
- `starter_profile` (Attributes) Create the profile from a predefined starter profile of transformation templates. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--source--starter_profile))

<a id="nestedatt--source--existing_versioned_profile_id"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source.existing_versioned_profile_id`

Optional:

- `profile_id` (String) The unique identifier of the source profile to clone.
- `version` (Number) The version number of the source profile to clone.


<a id="nestedatt--source--profile_mapping"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source.profile_mapping`

Optional:

- `profile_mapping` (Map of String) Map of template file paths to their Velocity template content.


<a id="nestedatt--source--starter_profile"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source.starter_profile`

Optional:

- `starter_profile_name` (String) The name of the starter profile to seed the profile from.



<a id="nestedatt--tags"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tags`

Optional:

- `key` (String) The key of the tag.
- `value` (String) The value of the tag.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_healthlake_data_transformation_profile.example
  identity = {
    arn = "arn"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the data transformation profile

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_healthlake_data_transformation_profile.example
  id = "arn"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_healthlake_data_transformation_profile.example "arn"
```
